_meta.purpose: Section-level landing-page patterns captured 1:1 from the Figma 'Landingpage - Blocks Pattern' catalog and the 'Landingpage - Examples' page. A pattern composes atomic blocks (landingpage/blocks.json) into a ready-to-place unit. `level` says whether the pattern IS a full Section (incl. wrapper) or is CONTENT to nest inside a titled Section.
_meta.source: Figma file uI6oKmJ0qDhsEuE0nFah2W, node 730:21779 (Landingpage - Blocks Pattern) → section 730:21788; hero + closing CTA from 362:1807 (Landingpage - Examples).
_meta.placeholderConvention: <...> = fill with real content; optional trailing '?' means the node may be dropped when empty. A '<a|b|c>' placeholder is a closed choice.
_meta.refConvention: { "$ref": "block.id" } expands to that block's `plan` fragment from landingpage/blocks.json. The agent expands $ref when authoring the Composition Plan — the render runtime never sees $ref.

landingpage.hero.purpose: Opening hero: a centered Heading followed by a button group — a Brand primary next to a Filled secondary. The example hero carries no description (heading + buttons only); add a short description only if needed.

landingpage.feature-grid.purpose: A grid of 2–4 teasers. Nest inside a titled Section (title = h2). Use image-top teasers (landingpage.image-teaser) or card teasers (landingpage.teaser-card). Auto equal-height.

landingpage.media-text-row.purpose: Media-text row, text-left: a 50-50 grid (gap xl) with the text block left and the image right. Nest inside a titled Section.

landingpage.media-text-row-reversed.purpose: Media-text row, image-left: same as media-text-row but the image leads and the text block follows. Alternate with media-text-row for rhythm.

landingpage.highlight-card.purpose: A single full-width elevation-1 Card containing a 50-50 grid: a text block (heading + description + Link) beside an Image. A featured highlight between grids/rows.

landingpage.cta-centered.purpose: Closing call-to-action: a centered Heading + a single Brand Button (the example). A Brand + Filled button group is the captured alternative (pattern 730:21794).
